summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined several recent developments in small UAS activity. Ukraine has unveiled the Gnome-NS, a new ground-based "mother drone" designed to carry and deploy FPV drones, and also introduced the Gnom-ND wheeled-tracked FPV carrier drone. Russian forces have deployed fiber-optic FPV drones in Kharkiv for the first time since the start of the full-scale invasion, and launched a major aerial attack ahead of Geneva talks with the US, including strikes on Perelyub village near Chernihiv and Orikhiv, where a 67-year-old was injured. Ukrainian forces have used FPV drones to knock out a Russian Kasta-2E2 radar in Zaporizhzhia and have developed the I-SEE system, which tracks Russian drones in real time without emitting radio signals. Motor-G is now shipping 200,000 drone motors per month, making it Ukraine's largest engine manufacturer, while Ukrainian BlueBird has updated its Gretz XL electronic warfare system to protect against Mavic drones. Additionally, Ukraine's $880M Drone Line has reportedly turned Russian frontline movement into a "death sentence" over the past 12 months, and a new interceptor by Ukrainian TAF Industries may appear on marketplaces in spring.
